sections.instruction: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.
Choose the correct letter A, B and C. Then label the map and complete the sentences as instructed.
sections.passage_or_context:
THE HISTORY OF ROSEWOOD HOUSE
RIVER WALK

questions.content: When the writer Sebastian George first saw Rosewood House, he
options: A thought he might rent it
B felt it was too expensive for him
C was unsure whether to buy it

questions.content: Before buying the house, George had
options: A experienced severe family problems
B struggled to become a successful author
C suffered a serious illness

questions.content: According to the speaker, George viewed Rosewood House as
options: A a rich source of material for his books
B a way to escape from his work
C a typical building of the region
A
B
C
D
E
F
G
H
I
J

sections.instruction: Complete the sentences and notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER for Questions 21-24, and NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for Questions 25-30.

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C for Questions 31-34. Complete the sentences below for Questions 35-40. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS each answer.

questions.content: According to the speaker, it is not clear
options: A when the farming economy was introduced to Ireland
B why people began to farm in Ireland
C where the early Irish farmers came from

questions.content: What point does the speaker make about breeding animals in Neolithic Ireland?
options: A Their numbers must have been above a certain level
B They were under threat from wild animals
C Some species died out during this period

questions.content: What does the speaker say about the transportation of animals?
options: A Livestock would have limited the distance the farmers could sail
B Neolithic boats were too primitive to have been used
C Probably only a few breeding animals were imported

questions.content: What is the main evidence for cereal crops in Neolithic Ireland?
options: A the remains of burnt grain in pots
B the marks left on pots by grains
C the patterns painted on the surface of pots

questions.content:
STONE TOOLS
35. Ploughs could either have been pulled by {{BLANK}} or by cattle.
• The farmers needed homes which were permanent dwellings.
36. In the final stages of axe-making {{BLANK}} and {{BLANK}} were necessary for grinding and polishing.
37. Irish axes were exported from Ireland to {{BLANK}} and England.

POTTERY MAKING
• The colonisers used clay to make pots.
38. The {{BLANK}} of the pots was often polished to make them watertight.
39. Clay from {{BLANK}} areas was generally used.
40. Decoration was only put around the {{BLANK}} of the earliest pots.
